commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Maintainers need to decide the bundled-provider boundary and recording-status protocol shape before an automated repair lane would be safe.; Needs attention: No supply-chain change stood out, but the Teams media bridge still needs a recording-status security/compliance gate before media-derived transcript persistence or response generation.; Review finding: [P1] Get bundled-provider ownership before exposing... (sha=73c715fbfca26a6aef345e75ada4ee50cbdf191e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a bundled `msteams` voice-call provider with an HMAC WebSocket bridge, SecretRef-backed config, streaming and realtime voice paths, secret-surface docs updates, and provider/transport tests.

PR surface: Source +1597, Tests +900, Docs +8. Total +2505 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR findings by source inspection: an `audio.frame` sent after `session.start` but before STT `connect()` resolves hits the `!isConnected()` branch and is dropped. The recording-status issue is also source-reproducible because transcripts are persisted and used without any recording-status state in the bridge protocol.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to decide the bundled-provider boundary and recording-status protocol shape before an automated repair lane would be safe.
